classes.name: Agent
classes.label: Agent
classes.comment: The human, group, or machine that consumes or acts upon an object or data. This higher-level grouping allows properties that are shared among its subclasses (Person, Organization, ....) to be anchored in one joint place, on the Agent class.

classes.name: Architecture
classes.label: Architecture
classes.comment: A designed/landscaped (or potentially designed/landscaped) part of the physical world that has a 3D spatial extent. E.g., a building site, a building, levels within the building, rooms, etc.

classes.name: ArchitectureArea
classes.label: Architecture area
classes.comment: Describes business-relevant area measurements typically associated with architected spaces. As the exact requirements on these measurements will vary from case to case or jurisdiction to jurisdiction, subclassing and specializing this definition is encouraged.

classes.name: ArchitectureCapacity
classes.label: Architecture capacity
classes.comment: Describes business-relevant capacity measurements typically associated with architected spaces. As the exact requirements on these measurements will vary from case to case or jurisdiction to jurisdiction, subclassing and specializing this definition is encouraged.

classes.name: Asset
classes.label: Asset
classes.comment: Something which is placed inside of a building, but is not an integral part of that building's structure; e.g., furniture, equipment, systems, etc.

classes.name: AudioVisualEquipment
classes.label: Audio Visual Equipment
classes.comment: Audio visual equipment.

classes.name: BACnetController
classes.label: BACnet Controller
classes.comment: BACnet controller.

classes.name: BooleanValueObservation
classes.label: Boolean value observation
classes.comment: Generic xsd:boolean value observation that is not specific to any particular QUDT quantitykind or unit.

classes.name: Building
classes.label: Building
classes.comment: A confined building structure.

classes.name: BuildingElement
classes.label: Building element
classes.comment: A part that constitutes a piece of a building's structural makeup. E.g., Facade, Wall, Slab, Roof, etc.

classes.name: Campus
classes.label: Campus
classes.comment: A campus represents a collection of location entities. The constituent locations may have differing legal ownership and utilization purposes, but they are generally perceived as a coherent unit or sub-region within a city or other region. E.g., a university campus, a hospital campus, a corporate campus, etc.

classes.name: Collection
classes.label: Collection
classes.comment: An administrative grouping of entities that are adressed and treated as a unit for some purpose. These entities may have some spatial arrangement (e.g., an apartment is typically contiguous) but that is not a requirement (see, e.g., a distributed campus consisting of spatially disjoint plots or buildings). Inclusion in a Collection is determined by the 'includes' field on a specific subclass.

classes.name: Controller
classes.label: Controller
classes.comment: Controller.

classes.name: DataNetworkEquipment
classes.label: Data Network Equipment
classes.comment: Data network equipment.

classes.name: DaylightSensorEquipment
classes.label: Daylight Sensor
classes.comment: Daylight sensor.

classes.name: DoubleValueObservation
classes.label: Double value observation
classes.comment: Generic xsd:double value observation that is not specific to any particular QUDT quantitykind or unit.

classes.name: EthernetPort
classes.label: Ethernet Port
classes.comment: Ethernet port.

classes.name: EthernetSwitch
classes.label: Ethernet Switch
classes.comment: Ethernet switch.

classes.name: Event
classes.label: Event
classes.comment: A temporally indexed entity, e.g., an observation, a lease, a construction project, etc. Can be instantaneous (timestamp property assigned) or have temporal extent (start and end properties assigned). Subclasses may define specific behaviour and requirements, e.g., on spatial indexing, agent participation, etc.

classes.name: Gateway
classes.label: Gateway
classes.comment: Gateway.

classes.name: Georeference
classes.label: Georeference
classes.comment: A georeference creates a relationship between a local coordinate system into a geographic coordinate system.

classes.name: Geotransform
classes.label: Geotransform
classes.comment: A transform following GDAL's Affine Transform that transforms a local coordinate into a WGS84 coordinate. Created from Ground Control Points (GCP) using GDAL's GCPsToGeotransform method.

classes.name: IAQSensorEquipment
classes.label: Indoor Air Quality Sensor
classes.comment: Indoor air quality sensor.

classes.name: ICTEquipment
classes.label: ICT Equipment
classes.comment: Equipment and devices that are part of a building's ICT infrastructure.

classes.name: ICTHardware
classes.label: ICT Hardware
classes.comment: ICT hardware.

classes.name: ITRack
classes.label: IT Rack
classes.comment: IT rack.

classes.name: IntegerValueObservation
classes.label: Integer value observation
classes.comment: Generic xsd:int value observation that is not specific to any particular QUDT quantitykind or unit.

classes.name: LeakDetectorEquipment
classes.label: Leak Detector
classes.comment: Leak detector.

classes.name: LeaseContract
classes.label: Lease contract
classes.comment: Formal document that identifies the Tenant and the leased asset or property; states lease term and fee (rent), and detailed terms and conditions of the lease agreement.

classes.name: Level
classes.label: Level
classes.comment: The level of a building, a.k.a. storey, floor, etc.

classes.name: ModbusController
classes.label: Modbus Controller
classes.comment: Modbus controller.

classes.name: NetworkRouter
classes.label: Network Router
classes.comment: Network router.

classes.name: NetworkSecurityEquipment
classes.label: Network Security Equipment
classes.comment: Network security equipment.

classes.name: OccupancySensorEquipment
classes.label: Occupancy Sensor
classes.comment: Occupancy sensor.

classes.name: OccupancyZone
classes.label: Occupancy zone
classes.comment: Occupancy Zone is a spatial area where devices are monitoring or reporting on the concept of Occupancy (motion sensors, people counters, cameras, etc.)

classes.name: Organization
classes.label: Organization
classes.comment: An organization of any sort (e.g., a business, association, project, consortium, tribe, etc.)

classes.name: PeopleCountSensorEquipment
classes.label: People Count Sensor
classes.comment: People count sensor.

classes.name: Person
classes.label: Person
classes.comment: A natural person (i.e., an individual human being).

classes.name: PointEvent
classes.label: Point event
classes.comment: An event emanating from or targeting a Point; e.g., an individual Observation from a Sensor point, or an Actuation sent to a Command point. In other terms, the Points indicate the capability of some Space or Equipment to emit or accept data, while this class represents those actual data messages. Note that in most non-trivially sized systems, these events are not stored in the knowledge graph itself, but are rather forwarded to some C&C system or time series database.

classes.name: Portfolio
classes.label: Portfolio
classes.comment: A portfolio is a grouping of buildings, sites, apartments, campuses, etc. that share some business-relevant commonality, e.g., are managed by the same company, are rented out to the same tenant, share utilization or legal definition (industrial vs commercial), etc.

classes.name: Premises
classes.label: Premises
classes.comment: A premises is an administrative grouping of spaces that are used for some commercial or industrial purpose by a real estate holder or tenant. E.g, a suite of offices, a shop, or an industrial workshop.

classes.name: RealEstate
classes.label: Real Estate
classes.comment: The legal/administrative representation of some lands and/or buildings. I.e., "Fastighet" (Swedish), "Ejendom" (Denmark), etc.

classes.name: Region
classes.label: Region
classes.comment: An administrative geospatial unit larger than the individual real estate. For instance, "Lombary", "North America", "The Back Bay", "Elnätsområde Syd", etc.

classes.name: SensorEquipment
classes.label: Sensor Equipment
classes.comment: Sensor equipment.

classes.name: Server
classes.label: Server
classes.comment: Server.

classes.name: Site
classes.label: Site
classes.comment: A piece of land upon which zero or more buildings may be situated.

classes.name: Space
classes.label: Space
classes.comment: A contiguous part of the physical world that contains or can contain sub-spaces. E.g., a Region can contain many Sites, which in turn can contain many Buildings.

classes.name: ThermostatEquipment
classes.label: Thermostat
classes.comment: Thermostat.

classes.name: VibrationSensorEquipment
classes.label: Vibration Sensor
classes.comment: Vibration sensor.

classes.name: WirelessAccessPoint
classes.label: Wireless Access Point
classes.comment: Wireless access point.

classes.name: Zone
classes.label: Zone
classes.comment: A sub-zone within or outside of a building defined to support some technology and/or use, e.g., an HVAC zone, a parking space, a security zone, etc.

object_properties.name: objectOf
object_properties.label: objectOf
object_properties.comment: The 'parent' BACnet device that hosts this BACnet object.

datatype_properties.name: BACnetURI
datatype_properties.label: BACnetURI
datatype_properties.comment: Clause Q.8 BACnet URI scheme: bacnet:// <device> / <object> [ / <property> [ / <index> ]]

datatype_properties.name: bacnet-read-property
datatype_properties.label: bacnet-read-property
datatype_properties.comment: The property of the BACnet object to read to get the current value of this entity.

datatype_properties.name: ifcFileLocation
datatype_properties.label: The location of the IFC file defining a project
